title: Add support for setting the CIDR when using slirp4netns
body:
This adds support for the --cidr parameter that is supported
by slirp4netns since v0.3.0. It allows the user to change the
IP range that is used for the network inside the container.

Changing the IP range can fix address conflicts with outside
networks or, which is the issue I had, with the virtualbox NAT
network mode which uses the same range (10.0.2.0/24).

This was referenced in https://github.com/rootless-containers/slirp4netns/issues/70 but
never got resolved.

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *hamzadis*, *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

This LGTM, but I would like to see this wired into containers.conf, so that users could permanently change this. I would also like to see the other slirp options available in containers.conf.

Then demonstrate the CIDR correctly via the --help, and hard code it in containers.conf rather then inside of the code.
